frontmatter.shortDescription: Self is a reference to the object on which the current method operates.
this is a special field in the object
Instance method or constructor call nodes in expression trees have a child labeled "this"
this is a local variable, also in static methods
In a constructor, this is null
The name this is not an expression

frontmatter.title: Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools
frontmatter.author: Alfred Aho, Ravi Sethi, Jeffrey Ullman, Monica S. Lam

frontmatter.title: nLab
frontmatter.author: David Corfield, David Roberts, Mike Shulman, Toby Bartels, Todd Trimble

pageContext.wikipediaSummary: this, self, and Me are keywords used in some computer programming languages to refer to the object, class, or other entity of which the currently running code is a part.  The entity referred to by these keywords thus depends on the execution context (such as which object is having its method called).  Different programming languages use these keywords in slightly different ways.  In languages where a keyword like "this" is mandatory, the keyword is the only way to access data and methods stored in the current object.  Where optional, they can disambiguate variables and functions with the same name.
